description: Minimal CMake C++ project (CMakeLists.txt + src/main.cpp) plus a qodana.yaml that enables the opt-in Misra inspection. CLion auto-detects CMake and resolves the model itself, then flags the planted defects. Asserts one finding per engine category, so a silently disabled category reds the cell: a use-after-move (Clang-Tidy umbrella id 'ClangTidy'), a const-able local (native AST 'CppLocalVariableMayBeConst', a Cpp* id only the paid CLion dist emits), and a non-zero octal literal (MISRA 'Misra', enabled via qodana.yaml). One further construct is planted but NOT asserted — a dead store (native DFA 'CppDFAUnusedValue') — because CLion drops it non-deterministically on fast machines: an async race in the Radler backend where inspection-session completion does not await the external DFA result (QD-15334; confirmed from run 28882514628 — present on arm64, absent on the faster amd64). The dead store stays planted so DFA coverage can be re-added once QD-15334 is fixed; asserting it now would rely on a data race. The image must carry the CLion dist's native deps (QD-15107): the .NET ReSharper backend's runtime libs (ICU/SSL/krb5) or it SIGABRTs at startup, and the JBR's libfreetype for the CMake console editor — either miss wedges the headless analyzer at 'Awaits CLion backend activities' (it HANGS instead of failing). No log gate on 'Problems found loading plugins:' — the CLion dist's bundled Angular/HtmlTools warning is benign and the source qodana-cli smoke test omits the same check. Guard (QD-14974): no 'unknown target triple' diagnostic poisons the TU. qodana-cpp (CLion/QDCPP, umbrella 'ClangTidy'/'Misra' + 'Cpp*' inspection ids) is distinct from qodana-clang (QDCLC, which emits per-check clang-tidy ids).

expectations.reason: The planted use-after-move. CLion runs clang-tidy and reports EVERY clang-tidy finding under the single umbrella inspection id 'ClangTidy' (NOT per-check ids like qodana-clang's 'bugprone-use-after-move'), so pin by ruleId=ClangTidy + the message. A silent zero means the analyzer found nothing (QD-14974). CI-pinned from run 27879871440.

Native-AST-category canary: CLion's deterministic non-DFA engine flags `taken` (never reassigned) — a Cpp* id only the paid CLion dist emits. Message verified from amd64 run 28862889738 ("Local variable 'taken' can be made const"); the exact id CLion's own qodana e2e pins under a default profile.

MISRA-category canary: the planted non-zero octal literal is the sole MISRA trigger in this TU, so ruleId 'Misra' on src/main.cpp is that finding (no messageContains — robust to the exact rule text/casing). MISRA is deterministic (an inspection enabled by name, not DFA), so a silent/broken MISRA reds the cell. CI-confirmed on both arches from run 28882514628 ('MISRA 5.13.2: Octal constants shall not be used'). The MISRA category silently produced nothing before QD-14509 (fixed in 262.SNAPSHOT.100 < our 263.130.1007).

A baked-in wrong target triple surfaces as an 'unknown target triple' diagnostic that poisons the whole TU (QD-14974). Asserted by message across ANY rule id (CLion does not surface it under a stable 'clang-diagnostic-error' id), so the positive findings above could not be produced if the TU were poisoned. Must be absent.
